thread_tweets.text:
The margin for tradfi assets are well calibrated with their volatility and liquidity. 20x on ES is fine. 

What perps provide is 50x on ES.  Just like bucket shops which were unregulated entities providing dangerous and well above tradfi leverage amounts 

I have no bias or position regarding Hyperliquid.  Nor am I in anyway in bed with tradfi. So my comments are not biased in any way due to vested interest.  How about you?
thread_tweets.full_text:

thread_tweets.text: @choffstein Hahaha bucket shops provide leverage that tradfi doesn't. Hyperliquid provides leverage that tradfi doesn't.
thread_tweets.full_text: @choffstein Hahaha bucket shops provide leverage that tradfi doesn't. Hyperliquid provides leverage that tradfi doesn't.

thread_tweets.text:
My literally only point is that perps offer more leverage than tradfi offers. Am I wrong.  You are trying to expand and distract from my point.  

Just to head off your next distraction of options.  Please stick to futures vs perps. 

As to institutional leverage in otc derivatives.  Sell side credit risk officers would not offer ISDA's to under capitalized highly leveraged specs and the terms and conditions and bankruptcy claims for margin calls are completely different than perps where unmet margin call losses are socialized

tweets.text:
What a remarkable outcome: the world's largest ever oil supply disruption failed to create a major energy crisis.

The IEA said 2026 shock was worst than 1973, 1979 and 2022 together. And yet, the cost of oil, natural gas, electricity and coal never surpassed the previous peaks.

Different point What is your thinking on asset prices? Specifically has government actions of debt increase fiscal side, and monetary actions on balance sheet and allowing inflation to stay above target been a wealth transfer to those who hold assets at the expense of those who don't including future generations of tax payers?

tweets.text:
Previous ATH now turned into strong support after several successful retests. 

You are doing yourself a disservice if you aren't focused on $HYPE HTFs. 

LTF noise is full of foolish leveraged traders, who in the years to come will be devastated that they fumbled Hyperliquid. 

If you are pattern matching other memecoins with zero flows and expecting the PA to match a project with ETF demand and 99% of revenue perpetually being used for buybacks then you are ngmi.

PA continues to resemble early BNB imo. 

Hyperliquid.

tweets.text:
Very sure anyone that sold off recent winners thinking they would buyback lower haven't managed to do so. 

This tweet remains very true. We are in the final innings of the bear. 

If you have identified potential winners over the next few years then all you have to do is hold on, and continue to monitor your thesis to ensure it remains true.  

Just as you can get too greedy at the top, you can get too greedy at the bottom. 

You don't get a 5-10x without being willing to stomach potential 30-40% drawdowns.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Can honestly say that trading assets in a bear market that I thought were great and going much higher at a later point was always a bad decision and a waste of time. 

Pendle my biggest example from the previous cycle.

You feel like a genius for a few weeks/months and then you

tweets.text:
Yep, very stupid to be a bear.

When Trump is doing everything to boost markets before Midterms. 

On top, your $WOLF power semi basket should go brrr from 800 VDC acceleration.

$LITE optical basket should go brrr from InP easing.  

$SPCX successful IPO gives more appetite to risk on themes/IPOs (eg. Space sector).  

And overall macro go brr from War/Strait peace deals.

It’s already kind of showing, since 2026 rate hike odds also crashed from 65% -> 35% following the news. Along with crude futures dropping. 

I've actually found Europe to be the most price sensitive to Iran tensions, so EU markets would probably be the most bullish overall… 

(South Korea/TW was originally with Sk Hynix moving directly in correlation to crude oil futures, but stopped caring after awhile).

But basically: Murica go brrrr.

tweets.text:
Today, there's a new report that China eased InP substrate exports. 

Which is expected to relieve mass production bottlenecks in the photonics market (source: Digitimes)

My optical positions are very happy to hear this:

From $AXTI (substrates), $IQE (epiwafers) to $AAOI (lasers) / $LITE / $SIVE, and others. 

Taiwanese optical players like VPEC, Landmark, and others should go brrr as well. 

Just to recap: the photonics market, especially for laser companies is moreso "how much can you make" rather than how much demand is there. 

InP substrates was one of the main bottlenecks affecting upstream capacity.   

So if you're able to make more = more revenue.

Today, there's a new report that China eased InP substrate exports. 

Which is expected to relieve mass production bottlenecks in the photonics market (source: Digitimes)

My optical positions are very happy to hear this:

From $AXTI (substrates), $IQE (epiwafers) to $AAOI (lasers) / $LITE / $SIVE, and others. 

Taiwanese optical players like VPEC, Landmark, and others should go brrr as well. 

Just to recap: the photonics market, especially for laser companies is moreso "how much can you make" rather than how much demand is there. 

InP substrates was one of the main bottlenecks affecting upstream capacity.   

So if you're able to make more = more revenue.

tweets.text:
$NVDA and $GOOGL lead 800V DC ahead of schedule. 

"Ahead of schedule", pulled up to Q3 2026 with small volume shipments starting . 

- Delta Electronics (2308), $VRT
- Song Chuan Precision (7788)
- Schneider Electric, Eaton, Siemens.

All flagged as beneficiaries. 

"Market sources indicate that Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform and Google’s next-generation AI data centers will be the first to adopt the technology"

Source: Commercial Times 

The power semi trade should be happy to hear this.

This is fascinating.

TL;DR

> Networks of smaller AI models are outperforming frontier models 
> The cheapest way to get Fable performance is any permutation of GPT and Fable
> The playbook is to take any frontier AI model, find the next-best (cheaper) frontier AI model, ensemble it with the leading open source model, and now you’ve got a cheaper version of the frontier
>  So called “Frontier AI Companies” will never again achieve the accuracy/cost/speed frontier
> The frontier is now owned by the network of leading models and companies
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
This is a *way* bigger deal than it seems... 

Frontier AI companies will *never* own the frontier again

I kid you not... I've been waiting for someone to show this result for like 4 years... this is a huge deal.

The short reason: combinations of models will *always* outperform

We just had the biggest Onchain AI catalysts last week
​
1. AI Censorship - frontier models ban → enterprises opt for open models  
2. Tokenoptimizing - Tokenmaxxing burn $$$ → open-source, DeAI offer cheaper alternatives  
3. Sovereign AI - nation developing own intelligence → more demand for open-source, DeAI
4. AI observability - observing & analyzing AI performance → leads to further optimization → more demand for open-source, DeAI
​
All roads lead to Decentralized AI

tweets.text:
the beautiful thing about this is satya’s AI vision is extremely pro-human

ai models don’t work without the humans guiding taste, setting goals, following instinct etc

the models are completely soulless without context and intents. thats why ai agents suck for most people right now. they’re lacking important instinct to WHY a human makes a decision in the workplace or at home. 

most people use ai as a google search replacement - the market to customise these models into a human co-worker is huge but difficult

the answer (in satyas vision) is the learning harness:

- combination of the ai model + context of work place + recursive learning loop

a self improving company.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: https://t.co/vLmiBKTtX3

tweets.text:
hang on this has got to be one of the most significant advances in AI recently - using a committee of (often dumber) models can achieve claude fable 5 performance for 50% of the cost but also beats fable?

its not because of the models themselves either, its the "judgement model" :

> openrouter's new fusion api basically sends your prompt to multiple models. a judgement model aggregates the answers then synthesizes the best answer

> the synthesis part is basically where the magic is. basically "two heads are better than one". 

> they made opus 4.8 talk to itself and that resulted in a 6 point boost in benchmark results.

its worth noting this wasn't achieved on long-horizon tasks (fable still smokes at this)

but for any company figuring out which model to use perhaps consider the answer might be *all of them*
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Introducing the Fusion API, the smartest compound model in the market.

Fusion achieves Fable-level intelligence at half the price.

How it works 👇 https://t.co/OTUQAdTQjU
